And lastly, the third 10 is that they have to feel connected to your company. They have to essentially trust and connect with the company itself. In other words, let's say a stock broker calls you up and he presents you with an unbelievable opportunity and he sounds great to himself. But you think his company might be a scammer illegitimate. You're not that sure. The question is, are you going to buy? Yes or no? The answer is you won't. Which is why so often we could be in a situation where our prospects are saying, oh my God, yeah, I love your idea. It sounds great. Oh, you're such a great guy too and yet for some reason they won't buy. And then yet if they were already a customer of yours and you had a relationship with them and you offered them basically the same opportunity, you would get a totally different response. They'd be like, oh my God, yeah, it sounds great. Jordan, do it. You see the difference? See, the reason why it's so much easier to make sales to existing customers than to new ones is because in the case of the form where you already have the second and third tens established. That's also why it's easy to get bigger sales from existing customers as opposed to when you first opened up the account. It's not an accident. You simply do not have these tens established with a new customer. So all you have to do with an existing customer, if you have a good relationship with them, is to get them to a 10 or even an eight about your product. So they're reasonably certain. But getting them to a 10, 10, 10, both logically and emotionally, this is what you're doing is you move them down the straight line. Make sense? Okay, good. So those are your first three elements. And when it's head to my sales, people, every sales the same, that's exactly what I was talking about. It doesn't matter what else is happening, these three things must line up in a single moment in time. In other words, you have to corral the three tens in a single moment in time and then ask for the order. If you have them at a 10, 10, 10, both logically and emotionally and ask for the order, then boom, you got a pretty good shot at closing them. That's why every sales the same. And the same thing needs to happen in any sale. And it doesn't matter what you're selling, if it's an idea, a vision, that I only have to love the vision, they also have to trust and connect with you as the visionary and also the organization behind you that's going to help you turn your vision into a reality. The same things have to align up no matter what the situation is.
